mappings.rationaleAI: Engineering covering machines and infrastructure connects to simple machines (B, B.1) and machine tools (C).
Autonomous machines in manufacturing address robots (D.3) and computer-integrated manufacturing (D.4).
Computing technology addresses computer numerical control (D.1) and CAD/CAM (D.2).
AI connects to robotic systems (D.3) and computer-integrated manufacturing (D.4).
Algorithms and programming underlie computer numerical control (D.1) and CAD/CAM (D.2).
Materials science connects to metal tool development (A.1), machine components (B), and machine tool characteristics and operation (C.1, C.2).
Nanoscale science connects to nonconventional machining methods (E) including electron-beam and ion beam machining (E.1).
Steam power and iron production trace machine tool history (C.1) and the transition from hand tools to machinery (A.1, B).
Prehistory addresses Paleolithic and Neolithic stone tools (A.1).
Industrial design connects to computer-aided design and manufacturing (D.2).
Structural engineering relates to machine components (B) and machine tools for construction (C).
Military innovation connects to early hand tool history (A.1) and machine tool development (C.1).
